name: Клятва Вероломства
description: The Oath of Treachery is the path followed by Paladins who have forsworn other oaths or who care only for their own power and survival. Commonly known as blackguards, these profane warriors are faithful only to themselves. This oath emphasizes self-preservation and the use of trickery and cunning to defeat foes.

features.name: Oath of Treachery Spells
features.description: The magic of your oath ensures you always have certain spells ready; when you reach a Paladin level specified in the Oath of Treachery Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

features.name: Conjure Duplicate
features.description: As an action, you can use your Channel Divinity to create a visual illusion of yourself that lasts for 1 minute, or until you lose {@status Concentration|XPHB} (as if you were concentrating on a spell). The illusion appears in an unoccupied space of your choice that you can see within 30 feet of you. The illusion looks exactly like you; it is silent; it is your size, is insubstantial, doesn't occupy its space, and is unaffected by attacks and damage. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} on your turn, you can move the illusion up to 30 feet to a space you can see, but the illusion must remain within 120 feet of you. During your turn, you can cast spells as though you were in the illusion's space, but you must use your own senses. Additionally, when both you and your illusion are within 5 feet of a creature that can see the illusion, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against that creature.

features.name: Poison Strike
features.description: You can use your Channel Divinity to make a weapon deadlier. As a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB}, you touch one weapon or piece of ammunition and conjure a special poison on it. The poison lasts for 1 minute. The next time you hit a target with an attack using that weapon or ammunition, the target takes Poison damage immediately after the attack. The Poison damage equals {@damage 2d10} plus your Paladin level, or {@damage 20} plus your Paladin level if you had {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the attack roll.

features.name: Aura of Treachery
features.description: You emanate an aura of discord, which gives you the following benefits. {@b Cull the Herd.} You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on melee attack rolls against any creature that has one or more of its allies within 5 feet of it. {@b Treacherous Strike.} If a creature within 5 feet of you misses you with a melee attack, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to force the attacker to reroll that attack against another creature of your choice that is also within 5 feet of the attacker. The ability fails and is wasted if the attacker is immune to being {@condition Charmed|XPHB}. You can use this ability three times. You regain expended uses of it when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

features.name: Blackguard's Escape
features.description: You have the ability to slip away from your foes. Immediately after you are hit by an attack, you can use your {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to turn {@condition Invisible|XPHB} and teleport up to 60 feet to a spot you can see. You remain {@condition Invisible|XPHB} until the end of your next turn or until you attack, deal damage, or force a creature to make a saving throw. Once you use this feature, you must finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} before you can use it again.

features.name: Icon of Deceit
features.description: You gain the ability to emanate feelings of treachery. As an action, you can magically become an avatar of deceit, gaining the following benefits for 1 minute. {@b Invisible.} You are {@condition Invisible|XPHB}. {@b Duplicates.} You create up to three illusory duplicates of yourself (as if you cast {@spell Mirror Image|XPHB}). When a duplicate is destroyed, you can move to the duplicate's space and end the invisibility. {@b Advantage.} You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
